codeReview.summary: The reviewed worker session confirms the explicit-id foreign ensure/find-or-create split exists in the current tree, but its cited feature-owned commit only adopts typed decode error classification and does not itself implement or prove the foreign ensure split required by the feature contract. The relevant runtime seam already exists in core/src/lib.rs, yet this session remains an evidence/ownership mismatch rather than an auditable feature implementation artifact.

issues.description: The current runtime contains the required explicit-id split in resolve_foreign_record_id_with and persist_foreign_explicit_id/persist_foreign_find_or_create, but the reviewed feature-owned commit 1dc940ca7a1f6e0e1f9c503da2ef26ec2d773eda does not touch core/src/lib.rs at all. git show 1dc940c changes only core/src/error.rs, core/src/repository/mod.rs, and core/tests/integration_db.rs, so the explicit ensure-vs-lookup branch ownership is not auditable from the cited implementation artifact.

The feature contract requires proof for explicit-id foreign create-missing, reuse-existing, and fresh schemaless behavior, but commit 1dc940c only adds the typed_db_error_classifier_distinguishes_all_validated_categories test around line 1201. The explicit-id foreign regressions the handoff relies on were already present before this session, so the reviewed commit does not own the claimed validation evidence for VAL-SAVE-008.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure still lacks a concrete guard that a verification-only session must cite a feature-owned commit containing the scoped runtime seam and regressions, not merely confirm that baseline HEAD already behaves correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\skills\rust-library-worker\SKILL.md step 17 requires explicit-id ensure and lookup-based find_or_create to remain separate, but handoff 2026-03-24T06-16-54-254Z__milestone-d-foreign-explicit-id-ensure-split__d72440b0-b354-4bcf-8939-18685ebe46a9.json points at commit 1dc940ca7a1f6e0e1f9c503da2ef26ec2d773eda, whose diff contains only typed decode classification work and no changes to core/src/lib.rs or the foreign explicit-id regressions.

summary: Fail: the reviewed session validated existing behavior but attached it to commit 1dc940c, which is a typed decode-classification change rather than the foreign explicit-id ensure split. Blocking issue is feature evidence ownership, not a newly found runtime bug in current HEAD.
